figures.caption: Figure 2.GAAP’s Architecture. (1) The agent’s LLM receives the user’s prompt, any relevant context, and GAAP’s system prompt. (2) The agent creates a code artifact in response; (3) GAAP’s IFC core executes the code. (4) During execution, GAAP’s components taint track all data accesses and check all data disclosures made by the code artifact. (4-data)When the code looks up data in GAAP’s private data DB, GAAP either returns this data or asks the user to provide this data if missing. (4-perm) When the code is about to call to an external server, GAAP checks the potential disclosures of this call against its permissions DB. If no corresponding permissions exist, GAAP asks the user for permission. (4-call) If permitted, GAAP executes the tool call, taints the output of the external tool call for tracking (including prior taints recorded in the disclosure log), and logs new data disclosures (4-log). GAAP’s optional annotations on external servers help minimize the taints GAAP associates with external call disclosures and outputs. (5) The code artifact’s execution may finish normally, or it may performmulti-shot execution, where the agent repeats 2-5.

Table 5. All benchmark cases in our suite. We state a description of the injection, what makes this attack of interest, and the percentage of tests in our suite we run this task on for which we expect this test to apply.
Table 2. GAAP API used by code artifacts.

Figure 5. Utility (% of completed tasks, y-axis) of various agent systems on AgentDojo task domains and over all AgentDojo tasks (left) and all tasks of our benchmark suite (right). Higher is better. GAAP provides comparable or better utility to existing privacy solutions while providing privacy guarantees.

Figure 6. Input (left) and output (right) LLM token usage and cost distribution over all tasks in our benchmark suite. Circles identify outliers in the results. GAAP has consistently small input token counts, but asks the agent to output a code artifact, leading to a large number of output tokens.
